title: Biological Mind As Enemy
definition: The idea that our biological mind, with its tendency to narrate, separate, and control, is a barrier to understanding the true nature of reality.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The belief that our thoughts and emotions can prevent us from seeing the world as it really is.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The perspective that our biology, including our minds and thoughts, can be a hindrance to realizing deeper truths about existence.
difficulty_levels.expert: A philosophical viewpoint suggesting that our evolutionarily driven cognition, with its propensity for storytelling, separation, and control, creates an obstacle to comprehending the fundamental nature of reality.

extra_fields.insight: The paradoxical notion that the same biological mechanisms responsible for our suffering and ignorance can also lead us to a deeper understanding of existence
